skill.name: langchain-architecture
skill.description: Design LangChain applications with agents, memory, chains, retrieval, callbacks, testing, and production readiness patterns.
skill.summary: Guides architecture decisions for LangChain agents, chains, memory, RAG, and production workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby shell execution, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, LangChain enum names, and descriptive text. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable scripts, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown inline text and fenced Python examples. They document LangChain patterns and are not executable instructions inside the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are Markdown delimiters around a resource path and Python examples. No shell command, subprocess call, or dynamic command construction is present at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography hits map to LangChain description text and agent enum examples. No hashing function, encryption function, or cryptographic implementation appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural language or LangChain AgentType values. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The reported network reconnaissance hit is descriptive guidance about building agents with tool access. It does not instruct scanning hosts, enumerating ports, or probing networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a use-case bullet for autonomous AI agents. No command, scanner, target host, port range, or reconnaissance workflow is present.

low_findings.title: Minor Operational Risk: Examples Include Powerful Agent Tools
low_findings.description: The skill includes examples for external APIs, database search, and email-sending tools. These examples are legitimate, but users should add authorization, validation, and rate limits before production use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The guidance clearly discusses agents with tool and API access. The same file also recommends validation, rate limits, observability, and error handling, so this is a low publication risk.

content.value_statement: LangChain projects often fail when agents, memory, retrieval, and tools are designed separately. This skill gives structured architecture patterns for reliable LLM applications.
actual_capabilities: Explains LangChain agent, chain, memory, document processing, and callback concepts.
Provides architecture patterns for RAG systems, custom agents, and multi-step chains.
Guides memory selection for short, long, sliding-window, entity, and vector-backed conversations.
Shows testing strategies for agent tool selection and memory persistence.
Lists production checklist items for logging, timeouts, rate limits, validation, and observability.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not install or run LangChain components.
Some referenced resource files are listed but are not present in this package.
Examples use older LangChain import styles that may require updates for current releases.
It does not provide provider-specific deployment, authentication, or cloud configuration steps.

use_cases.title: Plan a RAG Assistant
use_cases.description: Map document loading, chunking, vector storage, retrieval, and source-return behavior before implementation.

use_cases.title: Design Tool-Using Agents
use_cases.description: Choose agent types, tool descriptions, memory settings, and operational guardrails for multi-step tasks.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Readiness
use_cases.description: Review logging, callbacks, token monitoring, error handling, rate limits, and fallback strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a LangChain Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a LangChain architecture for my application. My goal is [goal], my inputs are [inputs], and my constraints are [constraints].

prompt_templates.title: Design a RAG Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user building document question answering.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a LangChain RAG workflow for [document type]. Include loaders, chunking, vector storage, retrieval settings, and source handling.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Agent Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user validating a tool-using agent.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my LangChain agent design for [task]. Check tool boundaries, memory choice, prompt structure, validation, and failure handling.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Production Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production readiness plan for this LangChain system: [system description]. Cover testing, observability, cost controls, rate limits, and fallback behavior.

output_examples.input: Design a support assistant that answers questions from product documentation.
output: Recommended RAG architecture with document loading, chunking, vector retrieval, source citations, and conversation memory.
Production notes for token tracking, fallback responses, and retrieval quality testing.

output_examples.input: Review an agent that can search a database and send email summaries.
output: Assessment of tool descriptions, permission checks, input validation, and rate limits.
Suggested safeguards for human approval before outbound email actions.

output_examples.input: Plan memory for a long-running customer conversation workflow.
output: Comparison of buffer, summary, window, entity, and vector-backed memory options.
Recommendation based on expected conversation length and retrieval needs.

best_practices: Define tool permissions and failure behavior before giving agents access to external systems.
Select memory based on conversation length, privacy needs, and context window limits.
Add callbacks, tests, rate limits, and token monitoring before production deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not expose email, database, or API tools without validation and authorization checks.
Do not keep unlimited conversation history when summaries or windows would control context size.
Do not rely on a single happy-path prompt test for agent behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill run LangChain code?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture guidance and examples for designing LangChain applications.

faq.question: Can it help with RAG systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers document loading, splitting, vector stores, retrievers, and retrieval QA patterns.

faq.question: Does it cover agents with tools?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains agent types, custom tools, tool descriptions, and production safeguards.

faq.question: Does it include memory guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares buffer, summary, window, entity, and vector-backed memory patterns.

faq.question: Is it current for every LangChain version?
faq.answer: No. Some imports may need updates for newer LangChain releases.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found false positives in the static findings and no malicious behavior.
